content_en:
Imam al-Juwayni, Abu al-Ma'ali Abd al-Malik ibn Abd Allah (d. 478 AH / 1085 CE), stands among the foremost Ash'ari theologians of the classical period. Born in Nishapur into a family of scholars, he studied under his father before traveling to Mecca, where he resided and taught for several years — earning him the epithet Imam al-Haramayn, the Imam of the Two Holy Cities. He later returned to Nishapur, where he led the Nizamiyyah academy and trained a generation of scholars that included Imam al-Ghazali. His mastery encompassed usul al-fiqh, theology, and jurisprudence, and he left behind works that shaped the course of Ash'ari thought for centuries.

Al-Irshad ila Qawati' al-Adilla fi Usul al-I'tiqad — The Guide to the Conclusive Proofs in the Fundamentals of Belief — is among al-Juwayni's most significant contributions to the science of kalam. It represents a systematic exposition of Ash'ari theology, articulating the rational foundations of Islamic belief in the divine attributes, prophethood, and the unseen. The Ash'ari school, founded by Imam Abu al-Hasan al-Ash'ari (d. 324 AH), affirms the divine attributes mentioned in the Quran and Sunnah while employing rational argumentation to defend them against philosophical objections. The Ash'ari and Maturidi schools together constitute the mainstream of Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah in matters of theology, and al-Juwayni's work stands as a foundational text within this tradition.

The book is organized according to the standard divisions of Ash'ari kalam. Al-Juwayni opens with proofs for the existence of the Creator derived from the contingency and temporal origination of the world, then proceeds through the divine attributes of life, knowledge, will, power, hearing, sight, and speech. He addresses the question of the divine essence and its relationship to the attributes with careful precision, and he devotes considerable attention to the principles governing prophethood — including the rational necessity of prophecy, the conditions of miracles, and the proofs establishing the prophethood of Muhammad, peace be upon him. Throughout, he employs the categories of necessary, impossible, and possible (wajib, mustahil, ja'iz) that became the backbone of classical Ash'ari pedagogy.

Among the key themes of Al-Irshad is its rigorous use of rational demonstration (burhan) without abandoning the primacy of revealed texts. Al-Juwayni consistently anchors his arguments in the Quran and the sound Sunnah while showing that reason, properly understood, supports rather than contradicts revelation. The work also engages extensively with the Mu'tazilah and other theological schools of his era, refuting their positions through both logical argument and textual evidence. This polemical dimension makes the book not only a statement of Ash'ari belief but also a defense of Ahl us-Sunnah against speculative deviations.

Readers approaching Al-Irshad should situate it within the broader landscape of Islamic theology. The Ash'ari school, including the work of al-Juwayni, is accepted as part of Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah, though the Athari approach — affirming the divine attributes as they appear in the texts without ta'wil — represents a distinct and equally authoritative position within the tradition. Scholars of all four madhabs have drawn on Ash'ari theology, and al-Juwayni's text in particular has served as a reference for students of usul al-din across the centuries. Engaging with this book offers the reader a window into the sophisticated intellectual tradition through which Muslim scholars defended the creed of Islam against its critics.
content_id:
Imam al-Juwayni, Abu al-Ma'ali Abd al-Malik ibn Abd Allah (w. 478 H / 1085 M), termasuk di antara teolog Asy'ari terkemuka pada periode klasik. Lahir di Nishapur dari keluarga ulama, beliau belajar kepada ayahnya sebelum melakukan perjalanan ke Mekah, tempat beliau tinggal dan mengajar selama beberapa tahun — sehingga mendapat gelar Imam al-Haramayn, Imam Dua Kota Suci. Beliau kemudian kembali ke Nishapur, memimpin akademi Nizamiyyah, dan mendidik generasi ulama yang mencakup Imam al-Ghazali. Keahliannya meliputi usul al-fiqh, teologi, dan fikih, dan beliau meninggalkan karya-karya yang membentuk perjalanan pemikiran Asy'ari selama berabad-abad.

Al-Irshad ila Qawati' al-Adilla fi Usul al-I'tiqad — Panduan menuju Dalil-Dalil yang Meyakinkan dalam Pokok-Pokok Akidah — termasuk salah satu sumbangan terpenting al-Juwayni bagi ilmu kalam. Karya ini merupakan uraian sistematis tentang teologi Asy'ari, yang memaparkan landasan rasional keimanan Islam tentang sifat-sifat Allah, kenabian, dan perkara-perkara gaib. Mazhab Asy'ari, yang didirikan oleh Imam Abu al-Hasan al-Asy'ari (w. 324 H), menetapkan sifat-sifat Allah yang disebutkan dalam Al-Quran dan Sunnah seraya menggunakan argumentasi rasional untuk mempertahankannya dari keberatan-keberatan filosofis. Mazhab Asy'ari dan Maturidi bersama-sama merupakan arus utama Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah dalam masalah teologi, dan karya al-Juwayni menjadi teks fundamental dalam tradisi ini.

Buku ini disusun menurut pembagian standar kalam Asy'ari. Al-Juwayni membuka dengan pembuktian keberadaan Pencipta yang berasal dari kontigensi dan sifat temporal alam semesta, kemudian meneruskan pembahasan tentang sifat-sifat Allah: hidup, ilmu, kehendak, kuasa, mendengar, melihat, dan berbicara. Beliau membahas persoalan zat Allah dan hubungannya dengan sifat-sifat dengan ketelitian tinggi, serta mencurahkan perhatian besar pada prinsip-prinsip kenabian — termasuk keniscayaan rasional kenabian, syarat-syarat mukjizat, dan dalil-dalil yang menetapkan kenabian Muhammad, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam. Sepanjang pembahasan, beliau menggunakan kategori wajib, mustahil, dan ja'iz yang menjadi tulang punggung pedagogi Asy'ari klasik.

Di antara tema utama Al-Irshad adalah penggunaan demonstrasi rasional (burhan) yang ketat tanpa meninggalkan keutamaan teks-teks wahyu. Al-Juwayni secara konsisten menyandarkan argumennya pada Al-Quran dan Sunnah yang sahih, sekaligus menunjukkan bahwa akal, jika dipahami dengan benar, mendukung wahyu bukan mengkontradiksinya. Karya ini juga banyak merespons Mu'tazilah dan mazhab-mazhab teologi lainnya pada zamannya, membantah posisi mereka melalui argumen logis maupun dalil tekstual. Dimensi polemis ini menjadikan buku ini bukan sekadar pernyataan keyakinan Asy'ari, tetapi juga pertahanan Ahl us-Sunnah terhadap penyimpangan spekulatif.

Pembaca yang mendekati Al-Irshad hendaknya menempatkannya dalam lanskap teologi Islam yang lebih luas. Mazhab Asy'ari, termasuk karya al-Juwayni, diterima sebagai bagian dari Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah, meski pendekatan Athari — menetapkan sifat-sifat Allah sebagaimana tampak dalam teks tanpa ta'wil — merupakan posisi yang berbeda namun sama-sama sahih dalam tradisi ini. Ulama dari keempat mazhab telah menimba dari teologi Asy'ari, dan teks al-Juwayni khususnya telah menjadi rujukan bagi para pelajar usul al-din lintas abad. Membaca karya ini memberikan pembaca jendela ke dalam tradisi intelektual yang canggih, tempat ulama Muslim mempertahankan akidah Islam dari para pengkritiknya.
